_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
procedure_name: Cerebral AVM embolization - Using Histoacryl (per sitting)
min_doctor_qualification: MCh/DNB/Equivalent in (Neurosurgery, if required Vascular Surgeon)
clinical_indications: Ruptured cerebral AVMs due to high rate of morbidity and mortality where the goal of treatment is eradication of the AVM

Complete resection or obliteration of the nidus and AV shunt, performed primarily or after endovascular embolization to reduce bleeding risks during surgery and to facilitate complete and uncomplicated removal

preauth.label: Clinical notes including neurological evaluation findings and planned line of treatment
Complete Blood Count, Creatinine, Prothrombin time (PT)/ INR

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes - detailed history, signs & symptoms, evaluation findings, indication for procedure, and planned line of treatment?
Did the patient present with epilepsy, severe headache, vomiting, loss of consciousness, neuro deficits (for Cerebral AVM)?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and line of treatment?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes available?
Was the imaging indicative of surgery?
Is the discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge?

common_queries: Clinical presentation, severity, or imaging not indicative of surgery.
Patient has evidence of unfavourable anatomy, renal insufficiency, contrast allergy, or non-correctable coagulopathy.
Clinical notes lack detailed history, signs & symptoms, evaluation findings, indication for procedure, or planned line of treatment.
Cerebral AVM patient presentation (epilepsy, severe headache, vomiting, loss of consciousness, neuro deficits) is not documented or does not meet criteria.
Imaging did not confirm the diagnosis.
Embolization material invoice/barcode not submitted with claim.

clinical_key_pointers: Cerebral arteriovenous malformations (cAVMs) are rare congenital anomalies of cerebral blood vessels resulting from maldevelopment of the capillary bed, permitting direct communication between cerebral arteries and veins.
Associated syndromes include hereditary hemorrhagic telangiectasia, Wyburn-Mason syndrome, von Hippel-Lindau disease, and Sturge-Weber syndrome.
Clinical presentation includes intracerebral hemorrhage (most common), seizure, headache, focal neurologic deficit, and incidental finding.
The Spetzler-Martin Scale is used to estimate the risk of surgical resection of an AVM, with higher grades associated with greater surgical morbidity and mortality.
Management of cerebral AVMs includes observation with medical management, microsurgical resection, stereotactic radiotherapy (SRS), and endovascular embolization.
